evals.input: Mine recent sessions for behavioral signals
evals.expected_output: Scans session JSONL files for corrections, directives, breakthroughs and returns mined findings
Returns the prioritized task list from task-list.json with scan results and pending work items
Executes finch.run: mines 24h of sessions, compacts MEMORY.md, routes findings, auto-applies low-risk items
Deduplicates, re-ranks, evicts stale entries, and compresses MEMORY.md

evals.input: Run memory_guard.py --apply on MEMORY.md that is 2,400 chars with 3 Always/Never directives and 2 see references/ pointer lines
evals.expected_output: MEMORY.md is at or under 2,200 chars post-write; all 3 directives are still present; pointer lines are removed; evicted lines are archived to memory_archive.md; a DecisionRecord is logged
evals.description: Guard cap enforcement + directive protection + pointer stripping

evals.input: Run memory_guard.py --apply twice on the same compliant MEMORY.md
evals.expected_output: Second run is byte-identical no-op (idempotent_noop=true); no backup churn; no archive entries added

evals.input: Run memory_guard.py --apply on MEMORY.md where directives alone exceed 2,200 chars
evals.expected_output: Guard refuses to evict directives; emits STILL OVER CAP warning; does not write; DecisionRecord logged with over_cap_after=true
evals.description: Guard refuses to evict directives past cap

evals.input: Kill memory_guard.py mid-write (SIGKILL during atomic_write), then inspect MEMORY.md
evals.expected_output: MEMORY.md is either the old content or the new content, never a partial write; .tmp file is cleaned up or .lock is stale-reclaimed
A new DecisionRecord entry exists with decision_type=memory_enforce, outcome=applied, and correct size/stripped/evicted counts
